quests.question: Convert to reported speech: He said, 'I am ready.'
options: He said that he was ready.
He said that he is ready.
He said that I was ready.
quests.hint: Remember to shift the tense backward (e.g., 'am' becomes 'was').
quests.explanation: In reported speech, we usually move the tense one step back into the past.

quests.question: Convert to reported speech: She told me, 'We will arrive soon.'
options: She told me that they would arrive soon.
She told me that they will arrive soon.
She told me that we would arrive soon.
quests.explanation: 'Will' shifts to 'would' in reported speech.

quests.question: The commander announced, 'I have finished the test.'
options: The commander announced that he had finished the test.
The commander announced that he finished the test.
The commander announced that he has finished the test.
quests.explanation: 'Have finished' shifts to 'had finished'.

quests.question: The pilot stated, 'The system is failing.'
options: The pilot stated that the system has failed.
The pilot stated that the system is failing.
The pilot stated that the system was failing.
quests.explanation: 'Is failing' shifts to 'was failing'.

quests.question: The doctor reported, 'I can help you.'
options: The doctor reported that he could help me.
The doctor reported that he can help me.
The doctor reported that I could help you.
quests.explanation: 'Can' shifts to 'could' in reported speech.

quests.question: The scientist explained, 'We saw the signal.'
options: The scientist explained that they have seen the signal.
The scientist explained that they had seen the signal.
The scientist explained that they saw the signal.
quests.explanation: 'Saw' shifts to 'had seen'.

quests.question: He said, 'I'm going to the store.'
options: He said that he was going to the store.
He said that he is going to the store.
He said that he goes to the store.
quests.explanation: 'I'm going' becomes 'he was going'.

quests.question: She asked, 'Are you happy?'
options: She asked if I am happy.
She asked if I was happy.
She asked was I happy.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'She asked if I was happy.'

quests.question: They told us, 'We have already left.'
options: They told us that they had already left.
They told us that they have already left.
They told us that they already left.
quests.explanation: 'Have left' becomes 'had left'.

quests.question: He stated, 'The project will be a success.'
options: He stated that the project would be a success.
He stated that the project will be a success.
He stated that the project was a success.
quests.explanation: 'Will be' shifts to 'would be'.

quests.question: She noted, 'I may come to the party.'
options: She noted that she might come to the party.
She noted that she may come to the party.
She noted that she would come to the party.
quests.explanation: 'May' becomes 'might' in reported speech.

quests.question: The teacher said, 'Finish your homework.'
options: The teacher said that we finished our homework.
The teacher told us to finish our homework.
The teacher said finish our homework.
quests.explanation: Commands are reported using 'told + object + to + infinitive'.

quests.question: He asked, 'Where are you going?'
options: He asked where was I going.
He asked where I am going.
He asked where I was going.
quests.hint: Question word followed by statement order.
quests.explanation: In reported questions, we use the word order of a statement.

quests.question: She warned, 'Don't touch the wire.'
options: She warned me not to touch the wire.
She warned me don't touch the wire.
She warned me to not touch the wire.
quests.explanation: Reported negative commands use 'warned + object + not to + infinitive'.

quests.question: They said, 'We are staying here.'
options: They said that they were staying there.
They said that they were staying here.
They said that they are staying here.
quests.explanation: Deictic words like 'here' shift to 'there' in reported speech.

quests.question: He asked her, 'Do you want some coffee?'
options: He asked her if she wanted some coffee.
He asked her if did she want some coffee.
He asked her whether she want some coffee.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'He asked her if she wanted some coffee.'

quests.question: She asked, 'What time does the train leave?'
options: She asked what time the train left.
She asked what time did the train leave.
She asked what time the train leaves.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'She asked what time the train left.'

quests.question: The detective asked, 'Where were you last night?'
options: The detective asked where I was the night before.
The detective asked where I had been the night before.
The detective asked where was I the night before.
quests.hint: Past simple shifts to past perfect.
quests.explanation: 'Were' shifts to 'had been' in reported questions.

quests.question: He told me, 'Open the window, please.'
options: He told me to open the window.
He asked me to open the window.
He said to me open the window.
quests.explanation: A polite request is best reported using 'asked + object + to + infinitive'.

quests.question: The officer ordered, 'Stand up immediately.'
options: The officer ordered us to stand up immediately.
The officer said that we stand up immediately.
The officer ordered that we stood up immediately.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'The officer ordered us to stand up immediately.'

quests.question: She advised, 'You should see a doctor.'
options: She advised me to see a doctor.
She advised that I see a doctor.
She said I should see a doctor.
quests.explanation: Advice is effectively reported using 'advised + object + to + infinitive'.

quests.question: He said, 'I must finish this report today.'
options: He said that he had to finish that report that day.
He said that he must finish this report today.
He said that he has to finish that report that day.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'He said that he had to finish that report that day.'

quests.question: She said, 'I might come if I have time.'
options: She said that she might come if she had time.
She said that she might come if she has time.
She said that she might have come if she had time.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'She said that she might come if she had time.'

quests.question: They said, 'We ought to leave early.'
options: They said that they ought to leave early.
They said that they ought to have left early.
They said that they had to leave early.
quests.explanation: 'Ought to' remains 'ought to' in reported speech.

quests.question: He promised, 'I will call you tomorrow.'
options: He promised that he would call me the next day.
He promised that he will call me tomorrow.
He promised that he would call me tomorrow.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'He promised that he would call me the next day.'

quests.question: She told him, 'I saw her two days ago.'
options: She told him that she had seen her two days ago.
She told him that she had seen her two days before.
She told him that she saw her two days before.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'She told him that she had seen her two days before.'

quests.question: They asked, 'Can we stay here tonight?'
options: They asked if they could stay here tonight.
They asked if they could stay there that night.
They asked could they stay there that night.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'They asked if they could stay there that night.'

quests.question: He said, 'I wish I were rich.'
options: He said that he wished he were rich.
He said that he wished he was rich.
He said that he had wished he were rich.
quests.explanation: The subjunctive 'were' often remains 'were' in reported speech after 'wish'.

quests.question: She remarked, 'If I had seen the sign, I would have stopped.'
options: She remarked that if she had seen the sign, she would have stopped.
She remarked that if she saw the sign, she would have stopped.
She remarked that if she had seen the sign, she would stop.
quests.hint: Third conditional does not shift further back.
quests.explanation: The third conditional 'had seen / would have stopped' remains unchanged.

quests.question: The captain shouted, 'Abandon ship at once!'
options: The captain shouted that we abandon ship at once.
The captain ordered to abandon ship at once.
The captain ordered us to abandon ship immediately.
quests.explanation: The correct reported form is: 'The captain ordered us to abandon ship immediately.'
